tts.instruction: Confident, calm hackathon narration. Native English product names. Slightly slower than chat.

chapters.title: One shared post before the team splits across tabs
chapters.body: Reports, watch terms, claims, and the decision trail meet inside Reddit while the incident is still moving.
chapters.narration: Live incidents fragment moderation in the first minute. Incident Room gives the team one shared command post inside Reddit.

chapters.title: Declare the incident, then work from the same room
chapters.body: The custom post shows status, score, evidence, active claims, briefing state, and the next moderator action.
chapters.narration: A moderator declares the incident. The room scores evidence, shows claims, and keeps the next action visible.

chapters.title: Declare, claim, brief, preview, confirm
chapters.body: Recorded from the running React app, using the same path covered by Playwright desktop and mobile tests.
chapters.narration: Here is the real flow: declare, claim evidence, open the briefing, preview an action pack, confirm it, then read the after-action record.

chapters.title: Rules score; Step 3.6 briefs; humans decide
chapters.body: Watch terms, reports, domains, and account age stay deterministic. The model explains the pattern and caveats only.
chapters.narration: Rules do the scoring. Step three point six writes the briefing only; it cannot mutate Reddit content or bypass moderator review.

chapters.title: Repo, review app, tests, and video are ready to inspect
chapters.body: The submission links the Devvit app, playtest subreddit, public repo, Step AI smoke test, and Playwright evidence.
chapters.narration: The repo, Devvit review app, tests, and video are linked in the submission. The first action is ready to inspect.
